accept: CLAUDE.md has a Documentation discipline section requiring asserting tests for user-visible behavior claims, with explicit examples of what counts (a help string, a CLAUDE.md instruction, a spec invariant) and what doesn't (an internal-only behavior detail).
At least one of the doc-vs-implementation sweep options is implemented and exercised on the existing codebase, surfacing any current drift as filed issues.

Test patterns from this session that would have caught the drift bugs (e.g. 'act show act-XX where XX is a unique 2-char prefix succeeds' for the prefix-matching case) are added to the test suite as regression coverage.
The discipline applies prospectively to every new doc claim added; not retroactive backfill.
payload.description:
We hit two doc-drift bugs that bit cold-start agents: 'prefix ok' was claimed in every help string but only full short ids worked (act-6fca), and CLAUDE.md's canonical loop didn't include git push (act-ac52). Common shape: doc claims behavior, no test asserts it against the implementation, drift goes unnoticed until an agent trips on it.

Two pieces needed:

**1. CLAUDE.md rule:** every user-visible behavior claim in a doc (act help, --help text, README, CLAUDE.md, spec) requires an asserting test that exercises the claimed behavior at the user-visible boundary, not at internal-state level. The double-prefix bug passed every internal test because tests checked op-file bytes; only a test asserting 'git log -1 --format=%s' would have caught it.

**2. Doc-vs-implementation sweep tooling:** something that surfaces drift without requiring a human to re-read every doc against current code. Could be:
- A periodic agent task (every N closes, run a sweep)
- A static analyzer that extracts behavior claims from docs and emits an inventory (then humans/agents verify each)
- A test category 'doc-claim-tests' that's enforced to exist whenever a doc adds a claim
- All of the above

Pick one or more, file follow-up implementation issues. The CLAUDE.md rule is small and immediate; the tooling is larger.
payload.title: process for keeping docs in sync with implementation (drift prevention)
